support.feedback.issueOpenedWithDiagnost: GitHub issue opened. Diagnostics copied to clipboard.
support.feedback.issueOpenedFallback: GitHub issue opened, but the text was too long to prefill. Paste diagnostics from clipboard or save them as a file and attach it.
support.dialog.reportDescription: Write a title and description, then open a GitHub issue. You can copy diagnostics and paste them into the issue.
support.dialog.aboutCopy: Fyler is a desktop app for merging PDFs and images into a single PDF. Add files, pick the pages you want, and export the final document.
support.dialog.issueDescriptionPlacehold: What you did, what you expected, and what actually happened.
support.dialog.issueHint: Both fields are required. When you open the issue, we automatically copy diagnostics to your clipboard.
emptyState.title: Drag PDFs or images here
emptyState.description: Drop files in this area or click to choose them from disk.
fileList.title: Files ({count})
pagePicker.title: Content
finalDocument.title: Final document
outputPanel.presets.original.label: Original
outputPanel.presets.original.description: No resize and no JPEG re-encoding.
outputPanel.presets.light.label: Light
outputPanel.presets.light.description: Only reduces heavily oversized images based on how they are rendered in the final PDF.
outputPanel.presets.balanced.label: Balanced
outputPanel.presets.balanced.description: General-purpose tradeoff: reduces images beyond the detail the final layout can actually use.
outputPanel.presets.compact.label: Compact
outputPanel.presets.compact.description: Targets much lighter PDFs by trimming excess detail more aggressively.
tooltips.dpiTitle: Reduce images based on the detail that is actually useful in the final PDF
tooltips.jpegTitle: Numeric quality used when an image is re-encoded as JPEG
tooltips.imageFitTitle: How the image is rendered on the final page
tooltips.imageFitItems.contain.title: Contain
tooltips.imageFitItems.contain.descripti: Uses an A4 page and fits the whole image inside, with white margins if needed.
tooltips.imageFitItems.fit.title: Fit
tooltips.imageFitItems.fit.description: The page matches the image size: everything stays visible, with no fixed A4 frame.
tooltips.imageFitItems.cover.title: Crop
tooltips.imageFitItems.cover.description: Uses a full A4 page: the image covers the whole page and excess parts are cropped.
tooltips.dpiDescriptions.220: Very conservative: only touches clearly oversized images.
tooltips.dpiDescriptions.170: Balanced tradeoff for documents meant to be shared and read on screen.
tooltips.dpiDescriptions.120: Reduces excess detail more aggressively to lighten the final PDF.
tooltips.jpegDescriptions.off: No manual override: uses the preset and target-DPI automatic behavior.
tooltips.jpegDescriptions.95: Very conservative quality, useful after a light resize.
tooltips.jpegDescriptions.90: Good tradeoff for general exports and sharing.
tooltips.jpegDescriptions.85: Reduces size more aggressively, with a higher chance of visible loss.
status.optimizationWarning.one: Export completed, but {count} image could not be optimized.
status.optimizationWarning.other: Export completed, but {count} images could not be optimized.
errors.source_not_found: A source file could not be found.
errors.page_missing_mediabox: PDF “{name}” contains a page without dimensions (MediaBox).
errors.skipped.path_error: Could not resolve a file path
pageSpec.outOfRange: Page {page} is out of range: the file has {total} pages.
update.title: Update available
update.message: Version {version} is available. Would you like to update now?
tutorial.step1: Here you'll find your files. You can add more or remove them.
tutorial.step2: Select the pages you want to include in the final document.
tutorial.step3: Reorder pages with the arrows or by dragging. Remove the ones you don't need.
tutorial.step4: When you're ready, export your PDF!
